subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze how science evolves with new evidence
Evaluate hypotheses, experiments, and conclusions critically
Understand and simulate peer review and collaboration
Apply scientific thinking to complex real-world problems
Identify errors, bias, and limitations in experiments
Develop higher-order reasoning and problem-solving skills

lesson_plan.title: Evolution of Scientific Knowledge
topics_covered: Examples of paradigm shifts in science
How theories change over time with new evidence
Historical case studies (e.g., heliocentric theory, germ theory)
teaching_strategy: Discussion on implications of changing theories
student_activities: Create a timeline showing changes in a scientific idea
Analyze why previous theories were replaced

lesson_plan.title: Critical Evaluation of Evidence
topics_covered: Difference between evidence, opinion, and assumption
teaching_strategy: Guided questioning on reliability and validity
student_activities: Identify potential errors and sources of bias

lesson_plan.title: Advanced Hypotheses and Experimentation
student_activities: Design and perform a controlled experiment
Record results and compare with peers

lesson_plan.title: Scientific Models and Theories – Analytical Approach
teaching_strategy: Demonstration with physical or visual models
Class discussion analyzing predictions vs observations

lesson_plan.title: Collaboration and Peer Review Simulation
topics_covered: Importance of collaboration in scientific advancement
teaching_strategy: Discussion on how peer review strengthens results
student_activities: Revise experimental conclusions based on feedback

lesson_plan.title: Revision, Analytical Quiz, and Real-World Application
topics_covered: Recap of scientific method, experiments, hypotheses, and theories
Application of scientific thinking to real-world issues
teaching_strategy: Quiz with complex scenarios and problem-solving
Class discussion of solutions and reasoning
student_activities: Relate scientific concepts to societal issues (health, technology, environment)

teaching_aids: Charts and visuals showing historical discoveries
teacher_tip: Focus on developing analytical reasoning, evaluating evidence critically, and linking scientific concepts to real-life applications. Encourage students to justify conclusions with evidence and explore alternative explanations.
